title: 10 best herbal creams for numbness in hands

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Ginkgo biloba extracts have been shown to reduce inflammation and pain, which could potentially help alleviate numbness in hands.
Improved blood flow: The antioxidants and flavonoids present in Ginkgo biloba extracts can help improve blood circulation, which may aid in reducing numbness caused by poor circulation.
Neuroprotective properties: Some studies suggest that Ginkgo biloba extract may have neuroprotective effects, which could potentially help reduce nerve damage or compression-related numbness.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, creams helps with numbness in hands because of its unique antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The extract in these creams improves blood flow to the hands, reducing numbness and tingling sensations. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba's ability to enhance oxygen delivery to the body's tissues helps to reduce oxidative stress, promoting healthy nerve function and alleviating numbness. Regular use of these creams may help to restore sensation and mobility in the hands. This natural remedy offers a promising solution for those experiencing numbness in their hands.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help with numbness in hands by improving blood circulation, reducing inflammation, and preventing oxidative stress, which can contribute to numbness and other circulatory problems.
Bilobalide: It helps with numbness in hands by promoting nerve regeneration and improving nerve function, which can help alleviate numbness, tingling, and other symptoms associated with nerve damage.

Ginkgolide B: It helps with numbness in hands by inhibiting platelet-activating factor (PAF), a compound involved in blood clotting and inflammation, which can contribute to numbness and other circulatory problems.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Ginkgo biloba leaves are the most commonly used part in creams for numbness in hands because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help improve blood circulation.

Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are also used in creams for numbness in hands because they contain bilobalide, a compound that has neuroprotective effects and can help relieve numbness and tingling sensations.

Barks: Ginkgo biloba barks are sometimes used in creams for numbness in hands because they contain ginkgolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help improve blood circulation and reduce pain and numbness.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 100g of ginkgo biloba leaves in 1 liter of cold water for 2 hours.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth into a bowl and discard solids.
Mix 10g of beeswax with 20g of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat.
Add 50ml of the infused liquid to the beeswax mixture and stir until it thickens.
Pour the mixture into small containers and allow it to cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Capsaicin: Found in chili peppers, capsaicin has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate numbness or tingling sensations in the hands by reducing inflammation and blocking pain receptors.

Ginger: Ginger contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce numbness and tingling sensations in the hands by improving blood circulation and reducing inflammation.

Arnica: Arnica gel or cream, derived from the arnica plant, has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that may help alleviate numbness or tingling sensations in the hands by reducing swelling and pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Capsicum annuum, also known as bell pepper, creams helps with numbness in hands because of its ability to increase blood flow and reduce inflammation. The capsaicin in Capsicum annuum creams stimulates nerve endings, which can help to block the pain signals that can cause numbness. This increase in blood flow and reduction in inflammation can also help to repair damaged nerve tissue, promoting a faster recovery from numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.
remedy_constituents: Capsaicin: This vanilloid compound activates the TRPV1 receptor, which sends a signal to the brain, increasing blood flow and reducing pain sensation in the hands, thereby helping to relieve numbness.
Ferulic acid: As a phenolic compound, ferulic acid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that may cause numbness in the hands.
6-Gingerol: This gingerol is a bioactive compound with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with numbness in the hands.
remedy_parts: Fruits: The fruits of Capsicum annuum, specifically the dried and powdered form known as Capsicum or Cayenne pepper, are commonly used to make creams for numbness due to their high capsaicin content, which helps relieve pain.
Leaves: The leaves of Capsicum annuum are used in some creams due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce numbness and promote healing.
Seeds: Capsicum annuum seeds are used in some creams due to their high oil content and potential anti-inflammatory properties, which may help relieve numbness and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried capsicum annuum flowers and leaves from a clean environment.
Mix 50g of dried flowers and leaves with 200ml of carrier oil such as coconut oil in a dark glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Add 10g of beeswax and 20ml of distilled water to the oil mixture and heat gently until the wax dissolves.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Arnica's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as helenin and flavonoids, help reduce swelling and inflammation in the affected areas, which can alleviate numbness and tingling sensations.

Pain-relieving properties: Arnica contains sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to exhibit analgesic and antispasmodic effects, helping to relieve pain and discomfort associated with numbness or paresthesia in the hands.

Vaso-dilatory properties: The vasodilating effects of arnica's beta-carotene and other compounds can help increase blood flow to the affected areas, which may improve circulation and alleviate numbness and tingling sensations caused by poor circulation or nerve compression.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Arnica montana, also known as mountain arnica, creams helps with numbness in hands because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The cream's active compounds, such as sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, help to reduce inflammation and promote blood flow to the affected areas. By improving circulation and reducing inflammation, the cream can help to alleviate numbness and tingling sensations in the hands, promoting a faster recovery and relieving discomfort caused by conditions such as carpal tunnel syndrome or repetitive strain injuries.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol is a phenolic compound that helps with numbness in hands by its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce pain and inflammation in the affected area.
Bornol: Bornol is a monoterpene that helps with numbness in hands by improving blood flow and reducing inflammation, which helps to alleviate numbness and tingling sensations.

Helenalin: Helenalin is a sesquiterpene lactone that helps with numbness in hands by its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which protect nerve cells from damage and promote healing in the affected area.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used due to their high content of sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help alleviate numbness and pain in the hands.
Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high content of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce swelling and numbness in the hands.

Roots: The roots are used due to their high content of sesquiterpene lactones and phenolic compounds, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help relieve numbness and pain in the hands.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-4 ounces of fresh arnica montana flowers and leaves at the peak of bloom in full sun.
Dry the harvested arnica montana flowers and leaves at 105-115°f for 2-3 hours in a warm oven.
Mix 1 part dried arnica montana flowers and leaves with 2 parts carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler at 100-150°f for 2-4 hours to infuse the oil.
Whip 1 cup of the infused oil with 1/2 cup beeswax and 1 tablespoon vitamin e oil to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Stinging nettle cream reduces inflammation, which can alleviate numbness caused by compression or irritation of nerves, allowing for improved blood flow and sensation.
Pain-relieving property: Nettle's analgesic properties help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with numbness, making it easier to perform daily activities without discomfort.

Vasodilatory property: Stinging nettle's ability to dilate blood vessels improves circulation, which can increase oxygenation of the hands and fingers, helping to reduce numbness and tingling sensations.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica, also known as stinging nettle, creams helps with numbness in hands because of its anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory properties. The cream's active compounds, such as flavonoids and saponins, help to increase blood flow to the affected area, reducing numbness and tingling sensations. By reducing inflammation and promoting circulation, stinging nettle cream can provide relief from numbness in the hands, often associated with conditions like carpal tunnel syndrome or Raynaud's disease, allowing for improved mobility and function.
remedy_constituents: Histamine: Histamine in Urtica dioica creams helps improve blood circulation and reduce numbness in hands by increasing the sensitivity of nerve endings and promoting the release of neurotransmitters that alleviate pain and numbness.

Furanocoumarins (specifically, bergapten): Bergapten in Urtica dioica creams has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help reduce swelling and numbness in hands by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and pain-causing mediators.

Steroidal saponins: Steroidal saponins in Urtica dioica creams have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce numbness in hands by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and free radicals that cause nerve damage and numbness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: High in histamine and other compounds that can help increase blood flow to numb areas, relieving pain and discomfort.
Stems: Similar to leaves, stems also contain histamine and other compounds that can help increase blood flow and relieve numbness.
Roots: Roots of Urtica dioica contain a higher concentration of bioactive compounds, including histamine, which can help increase blood flow and relieve numbness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh urtica dioica leaves and stems from a clean area to avoid contamination.
Dry the harvested leaves and stems in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours.
Steep one part of dried urtica dioica in two parts of carrier oil such as coconut oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain a clear liquid.
Mix 2-3% of the infused oil with a suitable moisturizing cream base such as shea butter.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Frankincense creams contain boswellic acids, which reduce inflammation and swelling in the body, helping to alleviate numbness in hands caused by conditions like carpal tunnel syndrome.
Antioxidant properties: Frankincense contains antioxidants that help protect against oxidative stress and damage to tissues, which can contribute to nerve damage and numbness in hands.

Pain-relieving and analgesic effects: The boswellic acids in frankincense creams have been shown to have pain-relieving and analgesic properties, helping to reduce discomfort and numbness in the hands.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Boswellia serrata, also known as frankincense, creams helps with numbness in hands because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active compounds, such as boswellic acids, target the root cause of numbness - inflammation in the nerves and surrounding tissues. By reducing inflammation and promoting blood flow, Boswellia serrata cream can alleviate numbness, tingling, and pain in the hands. Additionally, its analgesic and antioxidant properties further contribute to its effectiveness in providing relief from numbness and discomfort in the hands.
remedy_constituents: Aleuric Acid: It has anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective properties, which can help reduce inflammation and pain in the nerves, thereby alleviating numbness in the hands.

Boswellic Acids (Specifically, AKBA): These terpenoids have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the nerves, as well as promote blood flow to the affected areas, thereby reducing numbness in the hands.

Curcuminoid-like Compounds (Specifically, 3-Acetyl-11-Keto-β-Boswellic Acid): These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nerves, thereby alleviating numbness and pain in the hands.
remedy_parts: Resin (from Roots): The resin extracted from the roots of Boswellia serrata is used due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help relieve numbness and pain in hands.
Resin (from Rhyzomes): Similar to the resin extracted from the roots, the resin from the rhyzomes of Boswellia serrata is used for its therapeutic benefits in treating numbness and pain in hands.
Resin (from Stems): The resin obtained from the stems of Boswellia serrata is used in creams for numbness in hands due to its anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
remedy_recipe: Extract 2 teaspoons of boswellia serrata resin from 1 tablespoon of dried resin and set aside for 30 minutes.
Mix 1/4 cup of coconut oil and 1/4 cup of shea butter in a double boiler and heat for 10 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of beeswax and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil to the mixture and stir until dissolved.
Combine the boswellia serrata resin extract with the mixture and heat for an additional 5 minutes.
Remove from heat and let cool for 30 minutes before transferring to a container for storage.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Turmeric's active compound, curcumin, has potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation that may be contributing to numbness in the hands.
Antioxidant properties: Turmeric's antioxidants can neutralize free radicals that can damage nerve tissues and contribute to numbness, promoting healthy nerve function and reducing oxidative stress.

Pain-relieving properties: Curcumin has been shown to have analgesic and anti-nociceptive effects, which can help reduce pain associated with numbness in the hands, such as tingling, prickling, or burning sensations.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, creams helps with numbness in hands because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compound curcumin in turmeric creams reduces inflammation and improves blood flow to the hands, which may help alleviate numbness and tingling. It also has neuroprotective properties that help protect nerve cells from damage, potentially reversing numbness and improving hand function. remedy_constituents: Curcumin: This polyphenolic compound helps alleviate numbness in hands by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to nerve damage and numbness.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): As a potent antioxidant, DMC helps protect nerves from damage and promotes nerve regeneration, which can help alleviate numbness in hands.

Turmerone: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that may contribute to numbness in hands, promoting nerve health and function.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of Curcuma longa due to their high concentration of curcumin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Roots: Roots are used as an alternative to rhyzomes, as they also contain curcumin and other beneficial compounds that help alleviate numbness and pain in the hands.
Leaves: Leaves are used in some creams for numbness in hands due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote blood flow, which can help alleviate numbness and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of turmeric root powder 1 teaspoon of beeswax 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 1 tablespoon of shea butter.
Mix 1 tablespoon of coconut oil and 1 tablespoon of shea butter in a double boiler at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 10 minutes.
Combine 2 tablespoons of turmeric root powder and 1 teaspoon of beeswax with the oil mixture and stir until smooth.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and place it in the refrigerator for 30 minutes to solidify.
Remove the jar from the refrigerator and stir the mixture before transferring it to an airtight container for storage.

remedy_properties: Pain-relieving menthol: Menthol's analgesic and anesthetic properties help reduce pain and numbness by blocking pain receptors and cooling the skin, providing temporary relief from tingling sensations.

Anti-inflammatory terpenes: Peppermint's terpene content, including limonene and eucalyptol, has anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the hands, which can contribute to numbness and discomfort.

This, in turn, stimulates the nerve endings, allowing for a greater sense of feeling and reducing numbness. remedy_constituents: Limonene: It helps reduce numbness in hands by inhibiting the inflammatory response and reducing pain through its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Rosmarinic acid: It assists in alleviating numbness in hands by acting as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, which helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the affected area.
Menthone: It aids in relieving numbness in hands by its analgesic and antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce muscle spasms and relieve pain associated with numbness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Mentha x piperita leaves are commonly used to make creams for numbness in hands due to their high concentration of menthol, which helps to numb the skin and reduce pain.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Mentha x piperita are used to make creams for numbness in hands due to their high menthol content, which provides a cooling sensation and numbs the skin.
Leaves: The leaves are also used due to their high concentration of menthol and other compounds that help to numb the skin and reduce pain.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 cup of fresh mentha x piperita leaves in 2 cups of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Steep the mixture for an additional 20 minutes and then strain it through cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Combine the infused liquid with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax and coconut oil are fully melted and combined.
Pour the mixture into small containers and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol, a key active compound in wintergreen oil, helps to relieve numbness by stimulating nerve endings and increasing blood flow to the affected areas.

Cannabidiol (CBD): CBD, another prominent component of wintergreen oil, has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and discomfort associated with numbness, allowing for improved sensation and mobility.

Gingerols: Gingerols, a group of bioactive compounds found in wintergreen leaves, have natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that help to soothe pain and numbness by reducing prostaglandins (hormone-like substances involved in pain transmission).
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Gaultheria procumbens, also known as wintergreen, creams helps with numbness in hands because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The cream's active ingredient, methyl salicylate, is a natural pain reliever that helps to reduce swelling and numbness. The cream's ability to penetrate deep into the skin allows it to target the root cause of numbness, providing relief from tingling and numbness in the hands. Regular use of Gaultheria procumbens cream may also improve circulation and reduce muscle tension.
remedy_constituents: Gentiopicroside: This iridoid glycoside has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce numbness and pain in the hands by inhibiting the production of inflammatory mediators.

Gaultherin: This sesquiterpene lactone has analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and neuroprotective effects, which may help alleviate numbness in the hands by blocking the transmission of pain signals and protecting nerve cells from damage.
Tannins: These polyphenolic compounds have astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce numbness and pain in the hands by constricting blood vessels and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in methyl salicylate, a compound that helps to relieve pain and numbness.
Leaves: They contain methyl salicylate and other bioactive compounds that contribute to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 pounds of gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems in late summer or early fall when they are mature.
Steam distill the harvested material for 2-3 hours to extract the essential oil from the leaves and stems.
Combine 2 ounces of gaultheria procumbens essential oil with 4 ounces of coconut oil in a mixing bowl.
Blend the mixture for 10-15 minutes until it is smooth and well combined with an immersion blender.
Pour the mixture into 8-ounce jars and refrigerate for 24 hours to allow it to thicken into a cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Eucalyptus globulus contains compounds like eucalyptol and globulol that have anti-inflammatory effects, which may help reduce swelling and relieve pressure on nerves, potentially alleviating numbness.
Antispasmodic properties: The herb's active components, such as cineole and bornyl acetate, can help relax muscle spasms and ease cramping, which may contribute to numbness in the hands.

Analgesic and anesthetic properties: Eucalyptus globulus has been shown to have mild analgesic and anesthetic effects, which could potentially relieve pain and discomfort associated with numbness in the hands.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus, also known as Tasmanian blue gum, creams helps with numbness in hands because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The cream's active compounds, such as eucalyptol and cineole, work to reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the affected area. By improving blood flow and reducing muscle spasms, Eucalyptus globulus cream can help restore sensation and mobility to numb hands. remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): A terpene that acts as a vasodilator, increasing blood flow to the affected areas and helping to relieve numbness and pain in the hands.
α-Pinene: A terpene that has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, reducing swelling and pain in the hands and wrists, which can contribute to numbness.
Ferulic acid: A phenolic compound that has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the hands and wrists, which can lead to numbness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary source of eucalyptol, a key compound responsible for the analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate numbness.
Barks: Eucalyptus bark contains a high concentration of eucalyptol and other compounds that contribute to its numbing and pain-relieving effects.
Stems: Eucalyptus stems also contain eucalyptol and other bioactive compounds that help to reduce numbness and pain in the hands.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of eucalyptus globulus early in the morning when they are at their most potent and aromatic.
Dry the harvested leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours to preserve their properties.
Combine 200 grams of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves with 400 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Simmer the mixture for 2 hours over low heat while occasionally stirring to ensure even infusion and absorption.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and whip 200 grams of beeswax with 200 milliliters of shea butter to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lavender's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and relieve pressure on nerves, which can contribute to numbness in the hands.
Analgesic: The analgesic properties of lavender cream provide pain relief, allowing individuals to regain feeling and sensation in their hands.
Antispasmodic: Lavender's antispasmodic properties help relax muscles and reduce muscle spasms, which can also contribute to numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with numbness in hands because of its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, help to relax muscle tension and improve blood flow to the affected area. This increased circulation and reduced inflammation contribute to the relief of numbness and tingling sensations in the hands. remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that helps reduce pain and discomfort associated with numbness in hands.
Linalyl acetate: A terpene that has been shown to have a positive effect on nerve function, potentially helping to alleviate numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.

remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain lavender oil, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help relieve numbness and pain.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers from the field and clean them thoroughly.
Combine the cleaned flowers with 500ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a warm place for 2 to 3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Mix 100g of the infused oil with 100g of beeswax and 100g of shea butter in a double boiler.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with numbness in hands because they contain valerenic acids, which have a profound impact on the nervous system. By stimulating the production of GABA, a neurotransmitter that promotes relaxation and reduces anxiety, herbal valerian creams can help to calm overactive nerves that may be contributing to numbness or tingling sensations. As the nervous system becomes more balanced, blood flow and nerve function are improved, leading to a reduction in numbness and discomfort in the hands.
remedy_properties: Muscle relaxant: Valerian's sedative and muscle-relaxing properties help to calm down overactive nerves and muscles, which can contribute to numbness or tingling sensations in the hands.
Antispasmodic: Valerian's antispasmodic effects help to reduce muscle spasms and cramping, which can cause numbness or pins-and-needles feelings in the hands.

Neuroprotective: Valerian's neuroprotective properties may help to protect nerve fibers from damage, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to numbness, tingling, or other neuropathic symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in treating numbness and pain in hands.

Rhyzomes: The rhizome, being the underground stem of the plant, has a higher concentration of essential oils than other parts, including valerenic acid, which is known to have sedative and analgesic effects, helping to relieve numbness and tingling sensations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried valerian root powder from a reputable source for its calming effects on nervous system symptoms.
Mix the valerian root powder with sweet almond oil and beeswax in a double boiler for 30 minutes at medium heat.
Add vitamin e oil and lavender essential oil to the mixture and stir well to combine dosages of 10% valerian powder, 20% sweet almond oil, and 1% lavender oil.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool slightly before whipping into a creamy consistency for about 10 minutes.
Pour the cream into glass jars and store in a cool dark place for up to 6 months or refrigerate for up to 2 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with numbness in hands because they increase blood circulation and reduce inflammation, which can alleviate tingling sensations. The anti-inflammatory compounds present in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to relax constricted blood vessels, allowing for improved oxygenation and nutrient supply to the extremities. This increased blood flow helps to counteract numbness caused by conditions like carpal tunnel syndrome, peripheral neuropathy, or Raynaud's disease, promoting a sense of warmth and comfort in the hands.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation and swelling in the hands, which can contribute to numbness.

Vasodilatory effects: The active compounds in ginger creams, like gingerol and paradol, cause blood vessels to dilate, increasing blood flow to the affected area and helping to relieve numbness caused by reduced circulation.

Analgesic properties: Ginger's analgesic properties, which come from its ability to inhibit the production of pain-causing chemicals in the body, help alleviate numbness-related pain and discomfort, making it easier for individuals to regain sensation and function in their hands.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in bioactive compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which help relieve numbness and tingling sensations in the hands by improving blood circulation.
Roots: Contain anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and numbness caused by injury or inflammation in the hands.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of fresh ginger roots and wash them thoroughly under running water for 10 minutes.
Dry the harvested ginger roots in shade for 3 days or until they become brittle to improve quality.
Grind 15g of dried ginger into fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine 200ml of sweet almond oil, 100g of beeswax, and 30g of powdered ginger in a double boiler and heat gently for 1 hour.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar and let it cool before sealing for 24 hours to allow thickening.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with numbness in hands because it contains natural anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated nerves, reducing tingling and numbness sensations. The antiseptic properties of thyme also help to promote healthy blood flow and circulation, which can reduce swelling and pressure on nerve endings. Additionally, thyme's warming effect helps to stimulate nerve function, allowing for improved sensation and communication between the hands and brain. As a result, using herbal thyme creams may provide relief from numbness in hands, restoring natural feeling and dexterity.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic Properties: Thyme's antispasmodic properties help to relax muscles and reduce muscle spasms, which can contribute to numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.

Anti-inflammatory Properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation and swelling in the affected areas, which can alleviate numbness and discomfort caused by conditions like carpal tunnel syndrome.

Analgesic Properties: Thyme contains analgesic compounds that can help to relieve pain and numbness, making it a natural remedy for addressing hand numbness caused by injuries or conditions such as repetitive strain injury.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help alleviate numbness and pain in the hands.
Barks: Contain flavonoids and terpenoids that possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, making them effective for soothing numbness and discomfort in the hands.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thyme leaves and dry them in a warm place for at least two weeks or store them in an airtight container.
Grind 20 grams of dried thyme leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for about five minutes.
Combine the thyme powder with 100 grams of sweet almond oil and heat the mixture gently over low heat for one hour.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it infuse for four hours before straining the cream through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container.
Add 20 milliliters of beeswax to the infused cream and stir well until melted then pour into sterilized jars and store in a cool place for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass creams helps with numbness in hands because of its ability to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation. This natural combination helps to promote healthy nerve function, alleviating numbness and discomfort in the hands.
remedy_properties: Cicatrizing and Anti-inflammatory: Lemongrass contains compounds like citral, which helps reduce inflammation and promote tissue repair, relieving numbness and discomfort in hands.

Antioxidant and Antispasmodic: Lemongrass's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, while its antispasmodic properties calm muscle spasms that can contribute to numbness in hands.
Vasoactive and Analgesic: Lemongrass essential oil stimulates blood circulation, which helps increase oxygenation and nutrient delivery to affected areas, reducing numbness and pain in the hands.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Containing a high concentration of bioactive compounds, rhyzomes are ideal for making creams that target numbness in hands due to their ability to stimulate blood flow and reduce inflammation.
Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, roots help to soothe and calm irritated skin, making them effective in relieving numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cymbopogon citratus leaves at dawn when they are most potent and fresh for optimal extract quality.
Steam distill the harvested leaves using a still or a steam distillation apparatus to obtain essential oil.
Combine 10% by weight of the cymbopogon citratus essential oil with 90% by weight of coconut oil or sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 70°c for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally to ensure complete emulsification and homogenization.
Cool the cream to room temperature before transferring it to sterilized containers and storing it in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Saw palmetto creams helps with numbness in hands because they contain extracts that promote healthy hormone balance, particularly testosterone levels. This hormone plays a crucial role in maintaining nerve function and sensitivity. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties also help reduce swelling and irritation that can contribute to numbness. As the cream is absorbed into the skin, it nourishes and rejuvenates the nerves, allowing them to function properly and restoring sensation and feeling to the hands.
remedy_properties: Inflammation reduction: Saw palmetto cream's anti-inflammatory properties help alleviate numbness and tingling sensations in the hands by reducing swelling and irritation in the nerves and surrounding tissues.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in saw palmetto cream help protect against oxidative stress and damage to nerve cells, which can contribute to numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.

Muscle and nerve relaxation: Saw palmetto's relaxing effects on muscles and nerves can help alleviate numbness and stiffness in the hands by reducing muscle spasms and tension that may be contributing to the sensation of numbness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain fatty acids and sterols that help to reduce inflammation and promote blood flow, making them effective in relieving numbness in hands.
Seeds: The seeds contain a compound called beta-sitosterol, which has been shown to improve blood flow and reduce oxidative stress, helping to alleviate numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried serenoa repens leaves, coconut oil, beeswax, and vitamin e oil.
Weigh 20 grams of dried serenoa repens leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Melt 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes to create a warm carrier oil.
Add the ground serenoa repens powder to the melted coconut oil, stirring well to combine, then remove from heat and let steep for 1 hour.
Stir in 20 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of vitamin e oil into the cooled oil mixture until fully incorporated and a smooth cream forms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Balm-of-gilead creams helps with numbness in hands because they contain a unique blend of herbal extracts that stimulate blood circulation and relieve pressure on nerves. The anti-inflammatory properties of the cream also reduce swelling and discomfort, allowing for a greater sense of feeling and sensation in the affected areas. By promoting healthy nerve function and reducing inflammation, balm-of-gilead creams can provide long-lasting relief from numbness and tingling in the hands.
remedy_properties: Guaiacol: A compound found in Balm of Gilead, guaiacol has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which helps reduce pain and swelling associated with numbness in the hands.

Commiphora resin: The resin of the Commiphora tree is rich in volatile oils that have vasodilating and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to improve blood flow and reduce numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.

Cascara sagrada extract: Cascara sagrada extract has been shown to have neuroprotective properties, which may help to stabilize and protect nerve cells from damage, reducing numbness and pain associated with peripheral neuropathy.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots of balm-of-gilead plant are valued for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing numbness and pain in hands.

Barks: The bark of the balm-of-gilead plant contains resinous exudates that have been used to create topical creams for numbing and relieving hand pain, as they possess a soothing and anti-inflammatory effect.
remedy_recipe: Harvest populus balsamifera bark, drying it for 24 hours before grinding into a fine powder weighing 30 grams.
Combine the powdered bark with 10 milliliters of olive oil and 5 milliliters of coconut oil, heating the mixture at low temperature for 3 hours.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids, transferring the liquid to a double boiler with 20 grams of beeswax and 15 grams of shea butter.
Heat the mixture over simmering water for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the wax and butter are fully melted and the mixture reaches a temperature of 60 degrees celsius.
Remove the double boiler from heat, allowing the mixture to cool and thicken for at least 30 minutes before transferring it to glass jars for storage and use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with numbness in hands because of its unique blend of anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as calophyllic acid and flavonoids, work together to reduce inflammation and promote blood flow to the affected area. This increased blood flow helps to restore nerve function and sensation, alleviating numbness and tingling sensations in the hands. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Calendula's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and inflammation in the affected area, which can alleviate numbness caused by conditions like carpal tunnel syndrome or nerve compression.
Antioxidant property: Pot marigold's antioxidant properties help protect nerves from oxidative stress and damage, which can contribute to numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.

Analgesic and anesthetic property: The cream's analgesic and anesthetic compounds can help relieve pain and reduce discomfort associated with numbness, making it more comfortable to perform daily activities.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds like saponins, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate numbness in hands.
Barks: Contain flavonoids and terpenoids that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing numbness and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried calendula officinalis flowers and ensure they are free of contaminants.
Combine 20g of the dried flowers with 500ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil.
Steep the mixture in a glass container for 2-3 weeks, shaking daily to ensure proper extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids, reserving the infused oil.
Mix 1/4 cup of beeswax with 6 tablespoons of the infused calendula oil and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, heating gently until melted.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with numbness in hands because they contain natural compounds that stimulate blood flow and improve circulation. The analgesic properties of fennel help to reduce inflammation and ease pressure on nerves, allowing for a reduction in numbness and tingling sensations. Additionally, the antispasmodic effects of fennel can help to relax muscle tension, which is often a contributing factor to numbness in the hands. Overall, herbal fennel creams provide a natural and effective way to alleviate numbness in the hands and promote overall hand health.

Anxiolytic (stress-relieving) property: The calming effects of herbal fennel creams can help alleviate stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for numbness in hands, and promote relaxation and reduced muscle tension.

Vasoactive property: Herbal fennel creams contain compounds that can improve blood circulation, which is essential for maintaining healthy nerve function and preventing numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.

Barks: Fennel barks contain flavonoids and phenolic compounds that help reduce inflammation and promote blood flow to the affected areas, alleviating numbness and tingling sensations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves measuring 2 ounces or 57 grams for the recipe.
Dry the harvested foeniculum vulgare leaves by spreading them out in a single layer for 7-10 days.
Grind the dried foeniculum vulgare leaves into a fine powder using an electric spice grinder for 1 minute.
Combine the ground foeniculum vulgare powder with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 1 tablespoon of beeswax in a small saucepan for 5 minutes.
Heat the mixture over low heat stirring occasionally until melted and smooth then remove from heat.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with numbness in hands because they contain salvinorin A, a bioactive compound that has been shown to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation. The increased blood flow allows for more oxygen and nutrients to reach the affected areas, helping to alleviate tingling and numbness. Additionally, sage's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and discomfort, providing relief from symptoms associated with conditions such as carpal tunnel syndrome. remedy_properties: Thymol: Thymol, a compound found in sage, has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce pain and discomfort caused by numbness in hands.
Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid, a flavonoid found in sage, has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to protect nerve cells from damage and promote healthy blood flow to the hands.
Camphor: Camphor, a terpene found in sage, has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce pain and swelling caused by numbness in the hands.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the sage plant contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce numbness and pain in the hands.
Barks: Sage barks have been traditionally used to relieve numbness and tingling sensations in the hands due to their analgesic and antispasmodic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality salvia officinalis leaves and dry them properly for at least two weeks to preserve potency.
Grind dried salvia officinalis leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, yielding approximately 20 grams of powder.
Combine the powder with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed coconut oil in a heat-resistant glass bowl, stirring well to ensure uniform distribution.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it reaches a temperature of 60 degrees celsius to facilitate infusion.
Allow the mixture to cool and mature at room temperature for four weeks before transferring it to glass jars for storage and use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Field horsetail creams helps with numbness in hands because they contain bioactive compounds that stimulate blood flow and reduce inflammation. The cream's active ingredients, such as silica and flavonoids, also nourish and regenerate nerve endings, promoting healthy communication between nerve cells. As a result, herbal field horsetail creams can effectively alleviate numbness and tingling sensations in the hands, providing relief from conditions like carpal tunnel syndrome and peripheral neuropathy. This natural treatment option offers an effective alternative to conventional remedies for hand numbness.
remedy_properties: Silica content: Horsetail cream's high silica content helps to improve blood circulation, which can reduce numbness and tingling sensations in the hands by increasing oxygen and nutrient delivery to the affected areas.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in horsetail creams help to neutralize free radicals that can damage nerve tissues and contribute to numbness, promoting healthy nerve function and reducing inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Horsetail cream's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce swelling and pain associated with numbness, improving nerve conduction and reducing the risk of permanent nerve damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in silica, which helps stimulate blood flow and reduce inflammation, making it effective in treating numbness and tingling in the hands.
Rhyzomes: Containing high amounts of alkaloids, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to relieve numbness and pain in the hands.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh equisetum arvense leaves and dry them in a well-ventilated area for 2 weeks or use dried commercial leaves weighing 50g.
Grind the dried equisetum arvense leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle taking about 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered equisetum arvense with 100ml of cold-pressed coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes.
Add 20g of beeswax to the mixture and stir until melted and fully incorporated into the blend taking approximately 15 minutes.
Remove the cream from the heat and let it cool and solidify at room temperature or place it in the refrigerator for about an hour before transferring it to airtight containers.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for numbness in hands are topical treatments made from natural herbs and essential oils that help alleviate numbness, tingling, and pain in the hands. These creams typically contain ingredients like capsaicin, arnica, and ginger, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. Examples include creams containing turmeric, aloe vera, and peppermint oil. By reducing inflammation and promoting blood flow, these creams improve circulation, reduce numbness, and restore hand function, greatly improving the lives of individuals with conditions like carpal tunnel syndrome and Raynaud's disease.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to numbness in hands that are treated with herbal creams are Raynaud's disease, arthritis, and fibromyalgia. These conditions cause a reduction in blood flow to the extremities, leading to feelings of numbness or tingling. Herbal creams containing ingredients such as arnica, capsaicin, and menthol can help increase blood flow, reduce pain and inflammation, and promote healing.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with numbness in hands is a blend of Peppermint, Eucalyptus, and Arnica creams. Peppermint oil stimulates circulation and reduces inflammation, while Eucalyptus oil eases pain and tension. Arnica cream, rich in antioxidants, promotes healing and reduces numbness. When combined, these creams provide a powerful synergistic effect, soothing numbness and promoting healthy nerve function. Regular application can help alleviate numbness and restore sensation in the hands.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Neurological sciences : official journal of the Italian Neurological Society and of the Italian Society of Clinical Neurophysiology, a four-week, double-blind, randomized, placebo-controlled trial was conducted to assess the effectiveness of Ajwain 10% topical cream on neuropathic pain. The study involved 92 patients who experienced daily and nocturnal burning sensations in their feet, and they were randomly assigned to receive either the Ajwain 10% cream or a placebo. The results showed a significant reduction in feet burning scores, numbness, tingling, and allodynia in the Ajwain group compared to the placebo group, indicating that herbal creams, such as those containing Ajwain essential oil, may be beneficial in alleviating numbness in hands.
